modelingForm.kindHelp: What to build. Entity = a keyed, deduplicated dimension. Fact = measures reduced to a grain and joined to entity keys. Both become a materialized view.
modelingForm.nameHelp: The name of the entity or fact. It becomes the result table you query (e.g. Customer, Sales) and how other models reference it as a dimension.
modelingForm.sourceHelp: The registered table this model reads from. Its columns populate the pickers below.
modelingForm.domainHelp: The domain the new entity/fact is registered under — its namespace for access control and querying.
modelingForm.keyHint: Approximate numeric, JSON, and binary columns can't be a key (equality is unreliable).
modelingForm.keyHelp: The column(s) that identify one instance of this entity. Rows are deduplicated on it, and when history is tracked each change is versioned per key. It's also the join target facts reference.
modelingForm.attributesHelp: The descriptive columns carried alongside the key (name, region, tier …). Only these plus the key end up in the result; unselected columns are dropped.
modelingForm.historyHelp: How change over time is retained. none = current state only. SCD2 = each change is versioned per key, so you can query the value as of any past time. snapshot = the whole dataset is appended each refresh — full history, larger storage.
modelingForm.grainHelp: The column(s) that define a single fact row — the level of detail measures are aggregated to (e.g. order_id, or date+store). Coarser grain = fewer rows and more aggregation.
modelingForm.measuresHint: Only numeric columns can be aggregated.
modelingForm.measuresHelp: The numeric columns to aggregate to the grain, each with an aggregation (sum, avg, min, max, count). These become the fact's measure columns.
modelingForm.dimensionsHelp: The foreign-key links from this fact to entity dimensions: pick the referenced entity and the FK column that points to it. Each pair lets consumers join the fact to that dimension.
modelingForm.errorRequired: Name, source, and domain are required.
modelingForm.createdMv: Built a materialized view — the {{kind}} "{{name}}" in domain {{domain}}. It now appears in Registered Tables and is queryable.
modelingForm.createdHistory: History is tracked: past values are queryable via time travel.
modelingForm.createdDims: Registered {{count}} dimension link(s) so the fact can be joined to its entities.
modelingForm.reviseBody: This dialog only creates. To revise "{{name}}" — its key, attributes, history, or columns — edit it in Registered Tables. Re-running this dialog with the same name overwrites the existing model.
